calendar.matters:
Verified Petition For Approval Of Attorneys Fees And Costs As Part Of Conservatee'S Distribution In The Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-27-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jennifer Marlowe for Anthony Ongangco, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Gadi Zohar for Darren Wallace, Administrator.

Ms. Marlowe argued attorney fees and costs should be paid from the estate. She noted that at the October 15th hearing, the administrator proposed proceeding with distribution while withholding the amount requested for attorney fees, pending the outcome of this petition, and highlighted that requiring adjudication in San Mateo County would create unnecessary delays and costs. She asserted that this court is the appropriate forum for determining the reasonableness of attorney fees.

Mr. Zohar confirmed the agreement discussed at the October 15th hearing and stated that $160,000 has already been distributed to the conservatorship and the estate has sufficient funds to pay the attorney fees without prejudice. He made note that the administrator has no objection to the petition, and that the attorney for the conservatee has been notified and raised no concerns.

The court took the petition under submission for further review and consideration.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-16-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gadi Zohar for Warren King, Jr.
Jennifer Marlowe appeared by zoom for Anthony Ongcangco (objector/petitioner).

Mr. Zohar stated that he and Mrs. Marlowe have discussed this, that the objection is not really related to anything substantive with regard to this petition and that it has to do with another petition for Mrs. Marlowe's fees regarding litigation which is set for November 26th. Mr. Zohar also stated that the conservatorship is getting $197,778.96 either way, that it is a matter of how the money will be received, that the administrator could simply withhold part of the distribution to the conservator pending the result of the other petition or they can distribute the amount to the attorney and if the fee petition is denied, it can go directly to the conservator. In addition, Mr. Zohar stated that the urgency is that the beneficiary is coming in from Idaho to receive his check and do estate planning for his daughter. The Petition for Final Distribution to Beneficiaries and First and Final Report of Administrator, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Santos. Not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement Regarding Conservatee Flor E. King?S Interests In The Estate Of Warren King, Sr.
Off Calendar Aug-22-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Gadi Zohar appearing in person for Warren King Jr. (Petitioner).
Jennifer Marlowe appearing via zoom for and with Anthony Ongcangco (objector).

There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed. The will dated November 12, 2013 is admitted to probate. Darren Wallace is appointed as the Administrator with will annexed of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for September 24, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Shirly Yawitz is appointed Probate Referee.

Petition for approval of settlement agreement regarding conservatee Flor E. King's interest in the Estate of Warren King, Sr. is taken under submission with the intent to grant as presented.

Judge: Stephen Murphy: Clerk: Ateesh Datt: Deputy: Svahlin: Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-16-2023 Continued To Jun-27-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Gadi Zohar for appearing via zoom for Warren King, Jr. (petitioner).
Jennifer Marlowe appeared by zoom for Anthony Ongcangco.

The Parties reported they reached a settlement and that they need to due some tweaks to it. The parties also indicated that there is an issue on how Mr. Zohar's client will be appointed (executor/administrator) and that there may be a need to file for approval of comprise with respect to the real property. The Court informed the parties that it cannot agree to making a finding that the decedent died intestate when there is will. Mrs. Marlowe stated that she will be petitioning for court approval of the settlement agreement. The Petition for Probate of will and for letters testamentary, and status of mediation are continued to Jun 27, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mrs. Marlowe is to file her petition one week prior.

Judge; Stephen Murphy; Clerk: Ateesh Datt; Deputy: Cristobal; Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar May-16-2023 Continued To Jun-27-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-28-2023 Continued To May-16-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gadi Zohar for Warren King, Jr. (petitioner).
Jennifer Marlowe appeared by zoom for Anthony Ongcangco (temporary conservator of Flor King/contestant).
Jessica Rowe (court appointed counsel) for Flor King in San Mateo County.

The court stated that there are a number of issues with the temporary conservatorship of Flor King. Mrs. Marlowe stated that there is a hearing on the general appointment on Tuesday, that her client has the full support of the family, and that he does not have explicit authority to prosecute a will contest, but it can be cured when he gets appointed as a general conservator. Mr. Zohar stated that he waives formal service of the summons on behalf of his client, but he cannot waive formal service on behalf of his client's daughters, that the will contest is not actually a will contest, that she wants to admit the will to probate and that it should be brought under probate code section 11700 to determine succession of the estate property. Mr. Zohar also stated that the substance of the document is not contesting the validity of the will, that it is contesting the interpretation of the will. Mrs. Marlowe stated that the issue of their will contest cannot be decided without an evidentiary hearing, and suggested a continuance to meet and confer, and mediate. Further discussion was held regarding the issue of whether a guardian ad litem is needed, Mr. Zohar's objection to withdrawal of the petition for appointment of a Guardian Ad Litem, the issue of Anthony residing in the property rent free, a proposed settlement agreement having been sent on Friday, the conservator not being capable of acting in good faith, the difference in the role of court appointed counsel and a guardian ad litem, the issue of rents, the money received for the car that was sold being returned to the estate, Mrs. Marlowe filing a competing petition for the appointment of a professional, etc. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than May 2, 2023. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by May 9, 2023. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to and the Parties are to return on May 16, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The parties are to obtain a copy of the order referring the case to pro bono mediation from the court's website.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Malone. Not reported.
